title: High court magic politics
text: Shining capitals, formal magic schools, and court intrigue; power is public and dangerous to refuse.
examples: ballroom treaty that is a trap

title: Low fantasy mud and knives
text: Magic is rumor; problems are hunger, weather, and people with knives. Heroes look ordinary until cornered.
examples: a broken cart axle starts the plot

title: Sword and sorcery road
text: Wanderers, ruined idols, and short brutal victories; civilization is a tavern between wastelands.
examples: steal the idol, leave the town

title: Fair border bargain
text: Paths that shift after dusk; bargains with the fair folk cost names, shadows, or first harvests.

title: Dragon marches frontier
text: Border keeps where dragons are weather systems; ash on the wind means taxes and funerals.

title: Living dungeon ecology
text: Ruins that recycle explorers; fungus, traps, and territorial beasts form a working ecosystem.

title: Bronze-age mythic
text: Gods walk like weather; heroes are tools of curses and oaths more than free agents.

title: Clockwork fantasy city
text: Gears under cobbles, guild patents on timepieces, and magic that smells of oil.

title: Glass-spired academy
text: An academy of thin towers and stricter rivalries; exams can maim, tenure is politics.
examples: duel permitted in the east quad

title: Elfwood cutting edge
text: Where logging roads meet older trees that remember debts; guides charge in secrets.

title: Underhold trade gate
text: A stone gate city of ledgers and forge heat; clan seals open doors more than gold.

title: Crossroad inn of three banners
text: An inn where three petty realms pretend neutrality; every table has a spy or a poet.

title: Oracle cliff wind
text: A cliff shrine where answers arrive as weather; pilgrims leave tools, not coins.

title: Midnight goblin market
text: Stalls that sell impossible bargains; prices rearrange your memories.

title: Wizard tower rent flats
text: A tower subdivided into cheap flats; residual spells leak through floorboards.

title: College of Measured Flame
text: A magic college that licenses fire and prosecutes unlicensed miracles.

title: Order of the Quiet Shield
text: Knights who protect roads more than thrones; oaths are short and public.

title: Cistern thieves compact
text: A guild under the sewers that sells silence and map scraps; dues are favors.

title: Circle of Second Growth
text: Druids who rewild roads slowly and fine loggers in curses measured by acres.

title: Stubborn ember cantrip
text: A tiny flame that refuses fancy shapes; useful for wet tinder, useless as a weapon until trained.
examples: dry a sock, not a foe

title: Half-trust beast hush
text: Animals pause half a breath for your tone; they do not obey, only hesitate.

title: Chalk ward line
text: A chalk line that sometimes holds a lesser spirit; rain laughs at it.

title: Sword-form muscle echo
text: Body remembers one incomplete drill from a dead teacher; form breaks under real fear until practiced.

title: Herb-sigil mash
text: Mash leaves into temporary marks that nudge luck or itch; strength scales with correct plant, not willpower.

title: Coin-light flicker
text: Make a coin glow faintly; drains nothing important until overused, then steals sleep.
examples: read a map under a table

title: Wonder with receipts
text: Marvel is real and always billed later in debt, scars, or political obligation.

title: Mythic gravity
text: Language tilts epic without freeing the player from logistics and consequences.

title: Hired on at the caravan
text: Start as cheap road labor; the first problem is a missing mule, not a dark lord.
examples: mule tracks end at a shrine

title: Apprentice letter arrives late
text: A delayed letter names you apprentice to someone already dead; tools still wait in a locked shed.

title: Border gate stamp refused
text: Customs refuses your stamp; the line behind you decides whether you are a problem or a story.
examples: stamp ink is the wrong color
